Home

Cheese & Wine

The perfect pairing awaits

The Art of Cheese Making

Milk Collection

Fresh milk is collected from cows, goats, or sheep, depending on the cheese type.

Curdling

Rennet and starter cultures are added to curdle the milk into solid curds.

Cutting & Draining

Curds are cut and whey is drained, shaping the final cheese texture.

Aging

Cheeses are aged in controlled environments to develop flavor and character.

Popular French Cheeses

Île-de-France

Brie

Known as the "Queen of Cheeses," Brie is a soft cheese with a buttery flavor and creamy texture.

Soft textureMild flavorEdible rindAged 4-8 weeks
Normandy

Camembert

A soft, creamy cheese with an earthy aroma and rich, mushroom-like flavor.

Creamy interiorWhite rindEarthy aromaAged 3-5 weeks
Aveyron

Roquefort

A famous blue cheese made from sheep's milk with distinctive blue-green veins.

Blue veinsSharp tasteSheep milkAged in caves

French Wine Regions

France is one of the world's most renowned wine-producing countries. Each region has its own unique climate, soil (terroir), and grape varieties that create distinctive wines.

Bordeaux

One of the world's most famous wine regions, known for its full-bodied red blends.

Main Grapes

Cabernet SauvignonMerlotCabernet Franc

Full-bodied, complex, ages beautifully

Champagne

The only region that can produce true Champagne, known for its sparkling wines.

Main Grapes

ChardonnayPinot NoirPinot Meunier

Elegant, bubbly, celebratory

Burgundy

A prestigious region known for both exceptional reds (Pinot Noir) and whites (Chardonnay).

Main Grapes

Pinot NoirChardonnayGamay

Elegant, terroir-driven, refined

Classic Pairing Tip

"What grows together, goes together." Try pairing regional wines with local cheeses for the ultimate French experience.
